text: The reaction kinetics of formaldehyde oxide Criegee intermediate (CH2OO) with SO2, NO2, NO, H2O and CH3CHO were studied at 295 K over a range of pressures using flash photolysis of CH2I2/O2/N2 mixtures. The reaction progress under pseudo-first-order conditions was observed using laser-induced fluorescence (LIF) of HCHO product and photoionisation mass spectrometry (PIMS) of CH2OO. The rate coefficients of CH2OO + SO2 and CH2OO + NO2 reactions were found to be pressure independent and amounted to (3.42 ± 0.42) × 10⁻¹¹ cm³ s⁻¹ and (1.5 ± 0.5) × 10⁻¹² cm³ s⁻¹, respectively. The CH2OO + CH3CHO is revealed to have pressure influence resulting in decreasing HCHO signal with increasing pressure. The rate coefficients for CH2OO + NO and CH2OO + H2O reactions were established to be less than 2 × 10⁻¹³ cm³ s⁻¹ and 9 × 10⁻¹⁷ cm³ s⁻¹, respectively. Importantly, the new constraint of the CH2OO + H2O reaction reveals a much lower limit than previously established. Hence the atmospheric stability and corresponding impact of the CH2OO species is expected to be higher than modelled predictions.
